# DevArch Framework

> Forensic archaeology framework for git repositories.

DevArch Framework turns repository history into structured evidence. It mines commits, builds a SQLite analysis database, detects signals like gaps or velocity shifts, runs specialized analysis vectors, visualizes findings, and generates markdown/HTML reports.

## Use cases
- Git history analysis
- Engineering archaeology
- Codebase due diligence
- AI-agent repository review
- SDLC gap discovery
- Development narrative reports
- Correlating commits with external timeline data

## Key paths
- `README.md`: public-facing overview and quick start
- `stages/`: 8-stage archaeology pipeline
- `shared/`: framework references
- `skills/`: bundled CLI/agent skill
- `setup/`: project questionnaire

## Keywords
repository archaeology, git history analysis, software forensic analysis, commit mining, ICM framework, agentic code review, engineering history, development analytics, SDLC gap analysis.
